title: A Greedy Beaver Loves Snacks
summary: A greedy beaver was caught twice by rangers in Northumberland because it loved eating parsnips from a trap. The rangers set up traps with fruits and vegetables to check the health of beavers and put microchips on them. They wanted to make sure the beavers were healthy, especially the baby beavers called kits. The beaver family lives in an enclosure at Wallington, and the rangers use cameras to watch them. After catching the beaver dad twice, they plan to try again in September to catch more beavers. This story shows how animals can be curious and like tasty food, and how people work to protect them.

tiny devices put under skin to identify animals

options: It liked parsnips in the trap
It was looking for a mate
questions.correct_answer: It liked parsnips in the trap

options: Take them to a new home

questions.question: When will the rangers try to catch more beavers?

background_read: Beavers are animals that build dams in rivers and eat plants. Rangers are people who protect wildlife and help animals stay healthy. Microchips are small tools used to track pets or wild animals so we know who they are. This story happens in Northumberland, a place in England, where beavers are kept safe in an enclosure to study and care for them.
Article_Structure: WHO: National Trust rangers and a family of beavers
WHAT: Rangers caught a greedy beaver twice using traps with food to check health and microchip them
WHERE: Wallington enclosure in Northumberland, England
WHY: To monitor the beavers' health and identify them individually

perspectives.description: Shows how beavers can be curious and attracted to sweet foods like parsnips
Highlights how rangers work to protect and study beavers in a safe environment
